title: Cyanus eriophorus
taxonomy: Cyanus eriophorus is a member of the plant family Asteraceae. Within this family, it is classified as part of the tribe Cardueae. Further, it is placed in the subtribe Carduinae. The specific epithet 'eriophorus' is derived from the Greek words 'erion,' meaning wool, and 'phoros,' meaning bearing or carrying, which likely refers to the plant's hairy stems. This classification reflects the plant's botanical relationships and characteristics.
uses: Cyanus eriophorus, also known as the Corn Marigold, has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's flowers and leaves are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. Its antiseptic properties make it a potential natural remedy for preventing infections and promoting wound healing. Further research is needed to fully explore the medicinal potential of Cyanus eriophorus.
benefits: Cyanus eriophorus, commonly known as the corn marigold, has been found to have various health benefits due to its rich content of flavonoids and carotenoids. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and fever. Its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties make it effective in treating wounds and soothing irritated skin. Additionally, the plant has been studied for its potential to reduce oxidative stress and improve overall health. While more research is needed to confirm its full range of benefits, Cyanus eriophorus shows promise as a natural remedy for various health concerns.
properties: Cyanus eriophorus, commonly known as woolly meadow crane's-bill, has been used in traditional medicine for its therapeutic properties. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. It is also said to have antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against infections and promote healing. Additionally, the plant has been used to treat digestive issues and to reduce fever. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its therapeutic properties.
constituents: Cyanus eriophorus, commonly known as the corn marigold, is a plant species that contains various active constituents. These constituents include flavonoids, such as saponins and isoterpnenic acid, which contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. The plant also contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. Additionally, Cyanus eriophorus contains phenolic acids and other compounds that have antioxidant and antispasmodic effects. These active constituents make the plant a potential source of natural remedies for various health conditions.
parts: Cyanus eriophorus is a plant species also known as the red hawksbeard. The medicinal parts of Cyanus eriophorus are its leaves, flowers, and roots. Traditionally, these parts were used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and roots contain flavonoids and terpenes, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal applications of Cyanus eriophorus.
preparations: Cyanus eriophorus, also known as woolly meadow crane's-bill, is used in herbal preparations for its medicinal properties. The plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are used to create infusions, decoctions, and tinctures. In traditional medicine, the plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The herbal preparations made from Cyanus eriophorus are used to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cyanus eriophorus in herbal preparations.

intro: Cyanus eriophorus, also known as the Woolly Lettuce or Woolly Cudweed, is a perennial plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The Woolly Lettuce has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating various conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the Woolly Lettuce is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or dried powder, and is also used in skincare products due to its soothing and protective properties.
